place: DeKalb Junction CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: DeKalb Junction takes its name from American general Baron de Kalb, a German officer who served under George Washington.
The Town of DeKalb Historical Association opened in 1974, housed in a building that once served as a church.
Every year the association stages lectures, guided tours of local landmarks, and community festivals that celebrate the town’s heritage.
Its library holds a curated collection of books and documents detailing the region’s past, making it a key research spot for history buffs.
The association is a non‑profit organization based in DeKalb Junction, dedicated to preserving and sharing the town’s rich history.
